definition: Represent each image by scanner manufacturer/model, modality, site, protocol parameters, and acquisition settings when available. Compute exact-match or mixed metadata similarity to audit whether performance depends on scanner-domain overlap with the training set.
example_usage: python metadata_distance_similarity.py --train train_images.csv --eval eval_images.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --columns modality scanner_manufacturer site protocol

citations.title: The Impact of Scanner Domain Shift on Deep Learning Performance in Medical Imaging: an Experimental Study

evidence.summary: The paper shows scanner domain shift can substantially degrade medical-imaging model performance across modalities.
The study splits data by scanner domain and evaluates same-scanner versus different-scanner performance.
The appendix studies performance as target-domain images are progressively mixed into training.

limitations: Scanner metadata may be missing, anonymized, or inconsistent across datasets.
Exact manufacturer matching ignores continuous acquisition parameters and reconstruction software.
Site and scanner can be confounded with patient population or label prevalence.
quality_gates: Report scanner fields, modality, site, and any protocol parameters used.
Stratify by patient where applicable to avoid patient-level leakage.
Run a separate clinical-demographic or temporal axis when scanner is confounded with population.
